items.headline: Why is India blocking film on a man who counted Punjab insurgency killings?
items.detail: Satluj, based on life and murder of rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, is still finding its audience despite the ban.

items.headline: Trump threatens to attack ‘Pickaxe Mountain’ a nuclear facility inside Ira

items.headline: ‘Beijing is laughing’ at US-Mexico-Canada stalled trade talks
items.detail: A US trade expert says the US, Mexico, and Canada need to hold trilateral talks to get a cross-border trade deal signed.

items.headline: How US commerce secretary's Epstein links were uncovered by British whistleblower
items.detail: Simon Andriesz made the discovery about Howard Lutnick in publicly released Epstein files.

items.headline: Birthday present for Spain’s Yamal? World Cup semifinal win over France

items.headline: Death toll from Bangkok bar fire rises to 30 as police probe safety lapses
items.detail: Survivors and first reporters reported doors being locked and the lack of signage marking emergency exits.

items.headline: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.detail: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

items.headline: Australian police reveal unseen photos 25 years after British backpacker murder
items.detail: Police hope the images could jog memories that will help them find the body of Peter Falconio.

items.headline: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
items.detail: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

items.headline: X just tweaked its algorithm to make it more friendly, less battleground
items.detail: The social media site says it will amplify posts made by users' mutual followers' to give the feed more of a communal feel.

items.headline: California leads lawsuit to block Paramount Warner Bros mega merger
items.detail: The state, where the both studios have headquarters, has joined 11 others to halt the deal.

items.headline: Is tracking your food purchases good for your health?
items.detail: Food tracking apps are popular, but are they a good way to make us eat a healthier diet?

items.headline: 'I don't want to seem tight': How much should you give to the year-end teacher collection?
items.detail: Collecting money for a joint present might overcome the competitive gift-giving, but it can still cause friction.

items.headline: The secretive crypto firm backed by Farage's biggest donor
items.detail: Tether is a giant in crytocurrency, an industry that Nigel Farage believes the UK should be embracing.

items.headline: US judge voids Donald Trump's $1.8bn settlement with IRS that gave him immunity from tax audits
items.detail: The judge said the suit was brought for 'improper purposes' and referred a Trump attorney for possible disciplinary action.

items.headline: 10 years of Pokémon Go and the millions still trying to catch 'em all

items.headline: Meta pulls new AI image feature after days of backlash
items.detail: Meta's release this week of an AI feature that let people alter Instagram content drew swift blowback.

items.headline: Is the Assassin's Creed Black Flag remake worth the 13 year wait?
items.detail: The BBC's Tom Gerken plays the much-anticipated pirate game which has been remade from the ground up.
